fault_hypothesis: The overlap check on line 63 uses strict inequalities (`b <= s` and `a >= e`), treating touching boundaries (e.g., b == s or a == e) as non-overlapping. The tests expect touching boundaries to be treated as conflicts, so the conditions should be `b < s` and `a > e`.

spec.expr: not any(not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e) for s, e in timeline) or not any(b__1 == s or a__1 == e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: When the new window touches an existing slot boundary (b==s or a==e), the overlap check should detect a conflict; the current strict-inequality logic fails to do so.

spec.expr: not any(b__1 == s for s, e in timeline) or False

constraints.intent: Before using b__1 in the overlap check, assert that if b__1 equals any existing slot start, the check will correctly flag it as a conflict (which the current code does not).

spec.expr: not any(a__1 == e for s, e in timeline) or False

constraints.intent: Before using a__1 in the overlap check, assert that if a__1 equals any existing slot end, the check will correctly flag it as a conflict (which the current code does not).

spec.expr: all(b__1 < s or a__1 > e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: After passing the conflict check (else branch), every existing slot must be strictly non-touching with the new window; touching boundaries should have been caught.
